mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving sustained equity appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive 60-day forward return in at least 50% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation confirms healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, supporting positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of months where INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL growth remains below 0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll employment gains above 200,000 signal robust economic momentum, prompting algorithmic momentum strategies to bid up S&P 500 prices within five trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS readings above 200,000 correlate with negative 5-day forward returns in over 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling federal funds rates and rising real GDP growth creates a favorable liquidity and earnings environment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition of declining FEDFUNDS and positive GDPC1 growth yields negative 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production exceeding 0.5% month-over-month indicates severe supply-side weakness, forcing earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of months following an INDPRO decline greater than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in real personal consumption expenditures during high unemployment periods signal collapsing consumer confidence, triggering immediate short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS growth during UNRATE above 7.0% fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with positive returns occurring in over 55% of instances.
